phaseGoals.Create/Scaffold: Create the sample workspace, pull down the sample files, and initialize the azd project shape needed for the rest of the run.
phaseGoals.Provision: Provision the Azure-side infrastructure and verify that the expected account, project, model, and related resources exist.
phaseGoals.Local Run: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
phaseGoals.Deploy: Package the sample, push the image if needed, and register or deploy the hosted agent so the remote endpoint exists.
phaseGoals.Remote Invoke: Call the remotely deployed sample the way a user or engineer would and verify the result, latency, and first-use behavior.
phaseGoals.Monitor: Inspect the deployed agent, capture its metadata, and confirm whether the runtime looks healthy from a monitoring perspective.
phaseGoals.Update & Redeploy: Make a visible code change, redeploy it, and verify that the new behavior appears on a fresh remote execution path.
phaseGoals.Cleanup: Remove provisioned resources so the next run starts from a clean state and does not leak infrastructure.
